shortAnswer: Serum iron measures the amount of iron in the blood at the time of the blood draw. Low values may indicate iron deficiency; high values can suggest iron overload. Since levels fluctuate significantly, serum iron is always assessed together with ferritin and transferrin.
metaDescription: Serum iron elevated or low? Normal ranges, iron deficiency diagnosis, and meaning — explained simply.
doctorQuestions: Is there a true iron deficiency?
Should I be tested for hemochromatosis?
pageTitle: Fe value — Serum Iron explained | Medyra
